steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content:
Lines r and s are parallel, crossed by transversal t.

The angle 21° is formed at the intersection with line s on the left side of the transversal, below the line.

The angle x° is formed at the intersection with line r on the right side of the transversal, above the line.

These angles are on opposite sides of the transversal and both exterior to (outside) the parallel lines, making them alternate exterior angles.

steps.title: Apply the alternate exterior angles theorem
steps.content: When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, alternate exterior angles are congruent. Therefore: $$x° = 21°$$ which gives x = 21.
